best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback_long remains the core profit engine.
best_edges.evidence: 31 trades, $2294.6 PnL, 249.7 PnL/exposure hour, 93.5% win rate, 39.3% of latest total PnL despite worst MAE -392.

best_edges.edge: Fast winners are the strongest realized edge and should not be filtered by broad structure or VWAP rules.
best_edges.evidence: 57 fast winners generated $4856.7 with median MAE -29.75 and median hold 5.5m; broad hidden signatures retain only 70-88% PnL and leave worst MAE mostly unchanged.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze and charm_wall_breakaway are small but efficient continuation edges.
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long: 10 trades, $720, 186.9 PnL/hr, 100% win rate, worst MAE -208.25. charm_wall_breakaway_long: 5 trades, $517.6, 202.5 PnL/hr, median MFE 146.2, but tailier at -346.8 worst MAE.

best_edges.action: protect but do not tighten broadly
best_edges.edge: 1DTE breakout is profitable fallback insurance when 0DTE is unavailable.
best_edges.evidence: 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades, $1007.6 PnL, 95% win rate. Prior 1DTE quality-gate tests reduced score by -355 to -594 and cut net profit materially.

best_edges.edge: Call wall acceptance has a clean but one-trade sample.
best_edges.evidence: 1 trade, $91, 455 PnL/hr, -11.7 MAE, high ADX/setup quality. Prior loosening of call-wall gates hurt objective by -314 to -530 score, so expansion should be narrower than previous attempts.

broken_edges.evidence: Multiple fallback/no-0DTE tightening attempts were flat or negative and did not improve worst MAE. Latest stale/expired sample is profitable but tiny: 7 trades, $442 total fallback PnL.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback optimization is sample-starved and has repeatedly reduced objective when tightened.

broken_edges.evidence: Runtime health/tail containment improved worst MAE from -638.75 to -392 but cut score by -4668.6 and net profit by -544.2, with growth quality collapsing.
broken_edges.problem: Post-entry risk controls convert recoverable winners into objective loss under this no-stop strategy.

broken_edges.evidence: Inactive enabled profiles had zero trades in this window: wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long, late_day_pin_fade. breakout_long is disabled and also zero-trade.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or unreachable active router profiles should not receive threshold tuning until a routing/activation hypothesis is isolated.

broken_edges.evidence: Hidden signatures such as excluding breakout_long_1dte or structure/VWAP categories block 10-31 good trades, retain only 70.7-87.9% PnL, and generally leave worst MAE at -392.
broken_edges.problem: Most apparent bad-MAE filters are just broad profit reducers, not true tail isolators.

market_regime_read: The latest run is a profitable long-biased momentum/pullback regime but below the leader base. It produced 110 trades, $5835.2 PnL, 94.5% win rate, no slow >=12h trades, worst MAE -392, and 181.3 PnL/exposure hour. The leader base remains stronger at $7125.8, 196.98 PnL/exposure hour, score 9594.49, but with worse tail MAE -527. For profit expansion, the evidence says do not chase tail reduction unless it also preserves throughput. The real opportunity is restoring leader-like net PnL while protecting fast winners and avoiding previously failed 1DTE/call-wall/fallback broad tightening.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes meaningful PnL but breakout-only routing may be mixing pullback/reclaim states with continuation states.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Cannibalizes breakout_long_1dte winners or never routes if profile matching is too narrow.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Holdout
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: 1DTE option map, ES pullback/reclaim state, 0DTE unavailable state, profile-level PnL and MAE attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY with ES pullback/reclaim structure rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current 1DTE PnL, total PnL >=98% of leader base, and 1DTE average MAE improves versus -119.29.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Pullback_long is profitable but still owns the latest worst MAE and longest tail holds.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Existing config keys may not separate deep-heat pullbacks from profitable fast weak-structure pullbacks.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Split
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Entry-state pullback_long rows with early path diagnostics, veto reasons, structure distances, ADX slope, and time-to-profit labels.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long entries with weak structure, poor first move, low/negative ADX slope, and delayed profit behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE and PnL per exposure hour.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Dormant pullback_long_1dte is the only dormant one-dte concept directly aligned with observed 1DTE profitability.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May be unreachable or may steal high-quality breakout_long_1dte trades without reducing MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant pullback_long_1dte Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router one-dte profile attribution, 1DTE map state, ES pullback/reclaim state, per-profile trade count/PnL/MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: At least 5 routed trades or clear non-reachability signal; combined 1DTE PnL non-degrading and average MAE better than breakout_long_1dte alone.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Late-day/pin avoidance concepts may explain some high-MAE recoveries without bluntly blocking extended VWAP winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant profile activation can create low-frequency noise or conflict with existing charm/pin routing.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Holdout
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_0DTE pin proximity, charm wall, late-day context, rejected high/rolling-high features, profile routing attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: Pin/charm-wall risk context where active pullback/charm entries show delayed profit and elevated MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Positive standalone PnL, average MAE below active-profile median, and no reduction in protected fast-winner count.

next_experiments.config_bias: Expand only dynamic/take-profit or target capture logic on high-MFE profiles rather than relaxing entries. Favor profiles where median MFE materially exceeds configured TP or profile PnL/hour is already strong.
next_experiments.goal: Increase net PnL and PnL per exposure hour without increasing trade count noise.
next_experiments.hypothesis: squeeze_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long have enough MFE headroom to monetize more of existing winners, while preserving entry quality.
next_experiments.name: High-MFE Winner Monetization
next_experiments.primary_metric: Total net PnL and PnL/exposure hour, with worst MAE no worse than leader base and completed trades not materially reduced.
next_experiments.risk: Wider targets can lengthen holds and convert fast winners into capital-tied recoveries.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use surgical boundaries around the enabled extended-VWAP-rejection near-miss family only if the optimizer can preserve protected fast/extended-VWAP winners. Avoid broad setup, structure-score, or VWAP max-distance pruning.
next_experiments.goal: Trim the few worst pullback/charm-wall heat rows while retaining the fast-winner edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The latest worst-MAE rows are all covered by one near-miss veto family, but prior broad filters are too destructive; a narrow threshold nudge may improve risk-adjusted score if it blocks only the slow/deep-heat rows.
next_experiments.name: Extended-Rejection Near-Miss Micro Nudge
next_experiments.primary_metric: Risk-adjusted score, retained PnL >=98% of base, protected fast winners down <=2, worst MAE improves or average MAE improves without PnL/hour decay.
next_experiments.risk: The same signature includes profitable extended-VWAP winners, including one protected representative trade, so over-widening will cut the actual edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not repeat previous 1DTE tightening. Instead test a routing/sample split that adds a 1DTE pullback companion while leaving breakout_long_1dte quality mostly intact.
next_experiments.goal: Expand profitable 1DTE fallback participation or improve its MAE mix without damaging current 1DTE PnL.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY is profitable enough to preserve, but a companion pullback profile may route non-breakout 1DTE states more efficiently than tightening breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Routing Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, total score, 1DTE average MAE, and 1DTE PnL/exposure hour.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering may cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte trades; reject if total 1DTE PnL falls materially.

next_experiments.config_bias: Sample expansion should be stricter and more conditional than prior call-wall loosening: keep strong ADX/setup/acceptance context and avoid lowering several flow gates at once.
next_experiments.goal: Find whether call_wall_acceptance_long is a real scalable edge or just a one-trade artifact.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The one observed call-wall acceptance trade was exceptionally clean, but previous broad relaxation admitted worse opportunity cost; a narrower sample-builder may add a few clean trades without stealing pullback/squeeze edge.
next_experiments.name: Call-Wall Precision Sample Builder
next_experiments.primary_metric: At least 3-5 call_wall_acceptance_long trades, positive profile PnL, average MAE better than -60, and total PnL >=98% of leader base.
next_experiments.risk: Small-sample overfit and signal cannibalization from stronger active profiles.

next_experiments.config_bias: Restore profit-expansion posture by freezing failed risk exits and fallback edits; use leader-base behavior as the benchmark and avoid runtime health exits in this batch.
next_experiments.goal: Prevent optimizer from reintroducing known negative experiments while exploring profit expansion.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The largest recent score damage came from runtime risk containment and broad sample expansion, not from lack of entries.
next_experiments.name: Regression Guardrail Batch
next_experiments.primary_metric: Score stays near or above leader base, net PnL >= leader base, no increase in slow >=12h trades, and PnL/exposure hour >=197.
next_experiments.risk: Over-freezing may slow discovery, but evidence is strong enough to quarantine these families for the next batch.

research_notes: Use run_001/run_005 as the profit benchmark, not run_012. Latest run improved worst MAE but sacrificed too much growth quality and net profit, so the next research direction should restore throughput and monetize existing winners rather than adding more exit risk controls. The optimizer should prioritize experiments that can beat $7125.8 net PnL or 196.98 PnL/exposure hour while keeping latest-style MAE awareness. Avoid broad hidden-MAE signatures, broad 1DTE tightening, broad call-wall loosening, and no-0DTE fallback pruning because all have negative or weak historical evidence.
risk_controls: Reject any experiment that cuts total net PnL below 98% of leader base unless worst MAE improves dramatically and PnL/hour improves.
Reject broad entry filters that block more than 2-3 protected fast winners without a clear worst-MAE and score gain.
Keep slow >=12h trades at zero; any new profile activation must be isolated and attributable.
For 1DTE tests, measure combined 1DTE PnL, not just breakout_long_1dte standalone metrics, to avoid false wins from cannibalization.
Do not re-enable runtime stale/trade-health exits or broad adverse-point exits in profit-expansion mode without a separate risk-reducer mandate.
